objective: Build a deterministic autonomous system that converts high-level goals into executable infrastructure actions using multi-agent orchestration with Docker integration
decisions: Adopt layered architecture: main → orchestrator → agents → adapter → decision_engine → infra_manager → docker
Use FastAPI as control plane
Use Redis for state management
Use Prometheus for observability metrics
Implement multi-agent system with ScalerAgent, HealerAgent, OptimizerAgent
Introduce orchestrator as central routing mechanism
Implement goal planning engine to convert goals into execution plans
Standardize interface: async execute(context: dict)
Use adapter layer to decouple agents from decision_engine
Centralize infrastructure logic into infra_manager
Use docker compose for scaling workers
Use docker update for CPU optimization
Use docker restart for failure recovery
Implement dynamic container detection using docker ps
Store runtime context (latency, queue, failures) using Redis
Introduce debug logging for tracing execution
Adopt black formatter to eliminate syntax/indentation issues
Ensure deterministic routing (no randomness)
Maintain backward compatibility while stabilizing system
Avoid direct cross-layer dependencies
outputs: Docker infrastructure changes (scaled containers, CPU updates, restarts)
notes: System successfully executes goal → plan → agent → infrastructure actions with deterministic behavior. All major issues (interface mismatch, docker misconfiguration, syntax errors, data flow gaps) were resolved. Hardening completed via adapter layer, infra manager, logging, and formatting. Learning/memory layer (Phase 6) is designed but not implemented.
